''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]] was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut (Egyptian mythology)]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]]''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] [[sn:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]][[lt:Peremoudis]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G40-G1-N35-D21:N35:W24-G43-X1:X1
Pa-en-Renenutet
''P3-n-Rnnwtt''
''The (Month) of [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1 pr-D21:X1 N5
4-nu-peret
''4-nw-prt''
''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]] was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]][[lt:Peremoudis]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G40-G1-N35-D21:N35:W24-G43-X1:X1
Pa-en-Renenutet
''P3-n-Rnnwtt''
''The (Month) of [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1 pr-D21:X1 N5
4-nu-peret
''4-nw-prt''
''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]] was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]]''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}}

Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]]''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]][[lt:Peremoudis]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G40-G1-N35-D21:N35:W24-G43-X1:X1
Pa-en-Renenutet
''P3-n-Rnnwtt''
''The (Month) of [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1 pr-D21:X1 N5
4-nu-peret
''4-nw-prt''
''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]] was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]]''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]]''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]][[lt:Peremoudis]] [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]][[lt:Peremoudis]] [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]][[lt:Peremoudis]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G40-G1-N35-D21:N35:W24-G43-X1:X1
Pa-en-Renenutet
''P3-n-Rnnwtt''
''The (Month) of [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1 pr-D21:X1 N5
4-nu-peret
''4-nw-prt''
''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]] was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''[[:en:Mut|Mut]]''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]]''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the {{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Ancient Egyptian calendar}} for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === {{ill|Renenutet|en|Renenutet|WD=Q922680|lt=Renenutet}} was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === {{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|WD=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker}} suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''{{ill|Mut|en|Mut|WD=Q29409|lt=Mut}}''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''{{ill|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|en|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|WD=Q414163|lt=Academy of Sciences and Literature}}''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800|1=Tebtunis lunar calendar}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''{{ill|Mut|en|Mut|WD=Q29409|lt=Mut|1=Mut}}''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''{{ill|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|en|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|WD=Q414163|lt=Academy of Sciences and Literature|1=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}}''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

Pa-en-Renenutet
''P3-n-Rnnwtt''
''The (Month) of {{ill|Renenutet|en|Renenutet|WD=Q922680|lt=Renenutet|1=Renenutet}}'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1 pr-D21:X1 N5
4-nu-peret
''4-nw-prt''
''Fourth Month of the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} Season'' }} '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' (Greek: '''Pharmouthi'''; Coptic: '''Paremoude'''; Arabic: '''Baramouda''') was the term used in the {{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Ancient Egyptian calendar|1=Egyptian calendar}} for the fourth month of the season {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Jahreszeit)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, representing the period from '''mid-February to mid-March'''. == Background == === Mythology === {{ill|Renenutet|en|Renenutet|WD=Q922680|lt=Renenutet|1=Renenutet}} was revered as the '''"Lady of the Granary,"''' the '''"Lady of the Fertile Land,"''' and the '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing."''' During the harvest, Egyptians made offerings to her. Stelae in '''granaries and at field edges''' confirm the existence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the Barley"'''; in the '''Theban calendar''', it was observed on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1'''. As a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was responsible for the '''growth of flax.''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also held the titles '''"Lady of Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple Wardrobe"'''—a reference to the belief that '''the gods wore "the garments of Renenutet."''' === Change in Calendar System === {{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|WD=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner|1=Alan Gardiner}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}} suggest that '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' changed its position in the calendar over time. During the '''{{ill|19th Dynasty of Egypt|de|19. Dynastie (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', the month dedicated to '''Renutet''' was '''shifted from the ninth to the eighth month''' in the '''{{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|WD=Q180568|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}'''. In the '''{{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800|1=Tebtunis lunar calendar}}''' (ca. 132 BCE), '''Pa-en-Renenutet''' was referred to as '''{{ill|Mut|en|Mut|WD=Q29409|lt=Mut|1=Mut}}''' and spanned '''March 23 to April 20.''' This renaming was likely due to the '''{{ill|Day of the Birth of Mut|de|Tag des Gebärens der Mut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Birth of Mut|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''', which was celebrated on '''Pa-en-Renenutet 28'''. This festival '''symbolized the start of the harvest in March''', following the '''blessing of the fields''' by Renenutet. == References == * '''Rolf Krauss''': ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim, 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * '''Jürgen Osing''': ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis I'' (= ''CNI publications.'' Vol. 17/ ''The Carlsberg papyri.'' Vol. 2./ ''Carsten Niebuhr Institute publications.'' Vol. 17). Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies, University of Copenhagen/ Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen, 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * '''Richard Anthony Parker''': ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago, 1950. * '''Siegfried Schott''': ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der '''{{ill|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|en|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|WD=Q414163|lt=Academy of Sciences and Literature|1=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}}''', Mainz/Wiesbaden, 1950. : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Pa-en-Renenutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Pa-en-Renenutet]] [[lt:Peremoudis]] {{wikidata link|Q846942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
